metadata.role_title: Agentic AI Engineer / Backend Engineer (1.5-3 yrs)
metadata.funding_stage: Series A+ (March 2026, $14.5M led by Accenture, $250M valuation)
metadata.recent_signal: $14.5M Series A+ March 2026. CB Insights AI 100 2026. Launched Architect, Cognis, Diane in last 60 days.
metadata.match_reason: JD requires '1.5+ years building with LLMs and agent frameworks, RAG architectures, multi-agent systems, LangChain/LangGraph'. Onsite Bangalore.
metadata.cold_outreach_angle: LangChain + LangGraph agentic chatbot in production (Vivansh) + micro-agent orchestration across 3 LLMs (Form Builder). Directly matches what Lyzr builds. BGE-M3 + reranker work maps to their Cognis memory library architecture.
metadata.notes: $8M Series A Oct 2025 (Accenture, Rocketship.vc). Series A+ March 2026 at $250M valuation. Total funding $22.5M. On 2026 CB Insights AI 100. 51-200 employees.

research_summary: Lyzr AI builds a full-stack agentic AI platform for enterprises. $14.5M Series A+ March 2026 at $250M valuation, CB Insights AI 100 2026. Recent launches: Architect (AI app builder), Cognis (memory library with Matryoshka+BM25 RRF+BGE-2 reranker), Diane (HR AI agent), Lyzr ADK v0.1.8 with Agent Schedulers. 7 contacts identified: 3 senior AI/ML engineers (Tier 1), 2 junior/mid (Tier 3), 2 HR/recruiters (Tier 4). Email pattern confirmed as firstname@lyzr.ai. Strong technical overlap with Amit's LangChain+LangGraph agentic chatbot and BGE-M3+reranker RAG work.
jd_full_text:
Job Description - Agentic Ai Engineer | 2-3 Years of Experience
Onsite: Bangalore

About the role
We're looking for a full stack agentic AI engineer who has built production agentic AI systems - not just experimented with frameworks, but actually shipped things that work reliably at scale.
You'll be designing and building core agentic infrastructure, working directly with leadership to solve problems that don't have defined playbooks.

What you'll do
- Design and implement agent orchestration systems and multi-agent workflows
- Build scalable & reliable agentic systems
- Solve infrastructure challenges around execution, concurrency, and error handling
- Own problems end-to-end from architecture to production deployment

Requirements

Must have:
- 1.5+ years of dedicated hands-on experience building with LLMs and agent frameworks
- Production experience with agentic systems (Lyzr, LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, Google ADK, Claude Agent SDK etc.)
- Shipped products or systems that people actually use
- High ownership & High agency

Good to have:
- Startup or founding experience
- Open-source contributions to AI/agent frameworks
- ex-YC or native AI organizations

Why join
- Work on valuable unsolved problems in agentic AI
- Build something useful for potentially a million developers.
- Lean high ownership team that moves fast
tailoring_notes: 1) Professional Summary rewritten to lead with 'production agentic AI systems', LangChain/LangGraph, multi-agent orchestration, and 'high-ownership sole AI engineer' framing - mirrors JD language exactly. 2) Technical Skills reordered: Agent Frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, multi-agent, Claude Agent SDK) moved to first line; Hardware/IoT moved to last. LLMs + Retrieval in middle. 3) Work experience bullets unchanged - first Vivansh bullet already leads with LangChain/LangGraph agentic architecture. 4) Projects kept as-is (Voicer + WeepScope) - hardware projects demonstrate end-to-end ownership which JD values. IIT Delhi experience unchanged.

contacts.contact_role: Platform Engineer | Agentic AI Infrastructure @ Lyzr.ai | Ex-Senior Backend Engineer
contacts.research_hook: 1 month ago posted detailed breakdown of Cognis memory pipeline: Matryoshka vector search fused with BM25 through 70/30 RRF, BGE-2 cross-encoder reranker, 92.4% on LongMemEval. Near-identical retrieval stack to Amit's BGE-M3 + reranker production work at Silver Touch.
contacts.subject: your Cognis post on Matryoshka + BM25 RRF
contacts.content:
Hi Shusam,

Read your post last month on the Cognis memory pipeline. The 70/30 RRF fusion of Matryoshka vectors with BM25, then the BGE-2 cross-encoder reranker on top. That matched almost exactly what I built at Silver Touch: BGE-M3 dense plus sparse fused through a custom reranker, after testing 6 embedding models (BGE-small/base/large, Stella, SPLADE) on production-scale data. Different scale, same retrieval architecture.

Quick context: 2+ years building production agentic AI. At Vivansh now, I built a LangChain/LangGraph agent serving 500+ concurrent users with 250+ runtime permission checks per query. Before that, rebuilt RAG infra at Silver Touch from 45% to 95% MRR at 50,000+ queries/month.

Been following what Lyzr is shipping. The Architect, Cognis, and Diane launches in the last 60 days are interesting. If the team is hiring on the agentic AI/platform side, I'd like to be considered. Even a 15-minute call to hear how you think about the retrieval architecture in Cognis would be worth it.

Resume: amitgohel.dev

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your Cognis post on Matryoshka + BM25 RRF
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Shusam,

Bumping this up. Since I sent the last note, I went through the Lyzr ADK v0.1.8 changelog. The Agent Scheduler design for recurring and timed execution is something I'd want to dig into on a call.

If the timing is off, no problem.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your Cognis post on Matryoshka + BM25 RRF
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Shusam,

Last note from me. If Lyzr isn't hiring on the platform side right now, totally fine. Good luck with the Cognis roadmap.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.contact_role: Sr. Backend Engineer @ Lyrz AI | MTech CSE IIT Kanpur
contacts.research_hook: 5 months ago wrote a detailed post: 'Stop building monolithic AI agents. They are fragile. Old Way: 36.7% error rate in complex chains. New Way: 99.9997% success rate (Six Sigma).' Published a paper on decomposed micro-agent architecture. Maps directly to Amit's multi-agent orchestration with Pydantic-validated handoffs across 3 LLMs.
contacts.subject: your Six Sigma micro-agent post
contacts.content:
Hi Ayush,

Read your post on decomposed micro-agent architecture a while back. The numbers stuck: 36.7% error rate in monolithic chains, 99.9997% in the decomposed version. I built something that gets at the same problem differently. At Vivansh, I orchestrate 3 LLMs per form-processing request with Pydantic-validated handoffs between agents. The schema validation layer is exactly what kills the error cascade in long chains.

Quick context: 2+ years building production agentic AI. Built the LangChain/LangGraph agent at Vivansh (500+ concurrent users) and rebuilt RAG infra at Silver Touch from 45% to 95% MRR at 50,000+ queries/month.

Been tracking what Lyzr is building. If the team is hiring on the agentic side, I'd like to be in the mix. A short call would be useful.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your Six Sigma micro-agent post
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Ayush,

Following up. Since I sent this, I read through more of the micro-agent paper. The section on typed schema contracts between agents is close to what I'm doing in production with Pydantic. Would be good to compare notes if you have 15 minutes.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your Six Sigma micro-agent post
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Ayush,

Last note from me. If the timing isn't right, no worries. Good luck with the ADK roadmap.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.contact_role: Senior Software Engineer | Backend, Cloud & AI Systems | RAG & LLMs
contacts.research_hook: Posts regularly about Lyzr products. 2 days ago shared Diane HR agent launch. Strong backend + RAG + cloud systems focus - directly overlaps with Amit's Milvus RAG work, VLLM serving, and AWS infrastructure.
contacts.subject: question on Lyzr's RAG and cloud infra
contacts.content:
Hi Amish,

Saw your post on the Diane HR agent launch this week. The sourcing, screening, and scheduling automation is a good demo of what production agentic systems look like when they actually do something end to end.

Quick context: 2+ years on the AI/ML side. At Vivansh now, I run a LangChain/LangGraph agent on a multi-tenant EHS platform serving 500+ users, with a 2-stage Milvus retrieval pipeline enforcing 250+ granular permissions per query. Before that, rebuilt RAG infra at Silver Touch from 45% to 95% MRR across 50,000+ queries/month, including a full vector DB evaluation (Milvus vs. Qdrant vs. FAISS) and a custom BGE-M3 plus reranker pipeline. Infrastructure side: VLLM-served LLM API, AWS EC2/Lambda/S3.

The backend and cloud infra layer on top of the AI work is the part I'm most curious about at Lyzr. If the team is hiring, I'd like to be considered.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: question on Lyzr's RAG and cloud infra
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Amish,

Following up. Also saw the CB Insights AI 100 announcement this week. Good milestone for the team. Still interested in connecting if Lyzr is expanding on the AI infra side.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: question on Lyzr's RAG and cloud infra
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Amish,

Last one from me. If the timing is off, no problem. Good luck with the next product launch.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.contact_role: MTS @ Lyzr AI | Building Multi-Agent AI Products at Scale
contacts.research_hook: 6 days ago wrote a long post about Anthropic's Dreaming feature: 'We spent two years arguing about context windows and RAG strategies. Turns out the missing piece was not how much an agent could remember. It was whether the agent could forget the right things.' Connects to Amit's LangGraph memory and conversation management work.
contacts.subject: your post on Anthropic's Dreaming
contacts.content:
Hi Ishwar,

Read your post last week on Anthropic's Dreaming feature. The line that stuck: the missing piece wasn't how much an agent could remember, it was whether it could forget the right things. That's the exact tradeoff I run into at Vivansh with LangGraph conversation state. Keeping too much context creates retrieval noise; pruning too aggressively breaks continuity.

I'm an AI engineer, 2+ years in production. Built the LangChain/LangGraph agent at Vivansh (500+ concurrent users, 250+ permissions per query) and the RAG infra at Silver Touch (95% MRR, 50K queries/month). If Lyzr is hiring on the agentic AI side, I'd like to connect. Even a short conversation about how the team handles memory and context management in the ADK would be useful.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your post on Anthropic's Dreaming
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Ishwar,

Quick follow-up. Read your RLHF reward hacking post too. The goblin problem framing is a good way to think about misaligned agent behavior at inference time. Would be good to talk if the team is looking.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your post on Anthropic's Dreaming
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Ishwar,

Last note. If the timing isn't right, no worries.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.subject: your Series A+ post on enterprise AI audits

Hi Pranav,

Read your post after the Series A+ close. The part about 300-question technical audits, VAPT testing, and full VPC deployments before signing. That's the exact environment I was working in at Silver Touch: fully on-prem, zero external APIs, sensitive government documents with strict data sovereignty requirements. Not a demo, as you said.

I'm an AI engineer with 2+ years in production. Built on-prem OCR pipelines at Silver Touch and a full LangChain/LangGraph agentic system at Vivansh (500+ concurrent users, 95% MRR at 50K queries/month). If Lyzr is hiring on the agentic AI side, happy to connect.

Amit
